one_sentence_summary: God made humans to represent him in the world, and Jesus restores our true calling.
big_idea: Image of God: Because we are God’s image-bearers restored in Jesus, let’s reflect His character in how we treat people this week.

one_sentence_summary: God forms covenant partnerships to bless the world, ultimately fulfilled through Jesus.
big_idea: Covenants: Because God makes covenant partnerships to bless the world, let’s live as faithful partners who bring blessing to others this week.

one_sentence_summary: The Bible’s hope for a rescue-king culminates in Jesus’ surprising victory over evil.
big_idea: Messiah: Since Jesus is the Messiah who defeats evil through sacrificial love, let’s submit our plans to His kingship and follow His way this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s Spirit is his life-giving presence, empowering God’s people for new creation.
big_idea: Holy Spirit: Because the Holy Spirit is God’s empowering presence, let’s rely on His strength for courage, love, and witness this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s holiness is powerful goodness—dangerous to evil, healing to the humble.
big_idea: Holiness: Because God is holy—set-apart and healing—let’s turn from what corrupts us and draw near to Jesus for cleansing this week.

one_sentence_summary: Biblical justice is about making relationships right, especially for the vulnerable.
big_idea: Justice: Because God loves justice—right relationships—let’s take one concrete step to make things right for someone in need this week.

one_sentence_summary: God deals with evil and covers sin in a way that points forward to Jesus.
big_idea: Sacrifice & Atonement: Because Jesus’ sacrifice covers our sin and breaks evil’s power, let’s receive His forgiveness and extend forgiveness this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s goal is to reunite heaven and earth so he can dwell with humanity forever.
big_idea: Heaven & Earth: Because God’s goal is to unite heaven and earth, let’s bring a preview of His Kingdom by living with hope and peace this week.

video_name: Intro to the Sermon on the Mount
one_sentence_summary: Jesus announces God’s Kingdom and calls his followers into a new way of life.
big_idea: Sermon on the Mount: Because Jesus teaches the way of the Kingdom, let’s listen with humility and commit to practicing His words this week.

one_sentence_summary: Jesus describes the “good life” in God’s Kingdom—often opposite of the world’s values.
big_idea: Beatitudes: Because the ‘blessed life’ belongs to the humble and merciful in God’s Kingdom, let’s choose one Beatitude to practice this week.

one_sentence_summary: Jesus fulfills the Torah’s story and calls for a deeper kind of “doing right.”
big_idea: Jesus Fulfills the Law: Since Jesus fulfills the Law and calls for deeper righteousness, let’s pursue heart-level obedience shaped by love this week.

one_sentence_summary: Kingdom integrity is simple: become the kind of person whose “yes” and “no” can be trusted.
big_idea: Integrity: Because Kingdom integrity means our words can be trusted, let’s let our “yes” be yes in one specific conversation this week.

video_name: Wisdom Within Laws About Oaths, Retaliation, and Enemy Love
one_sentence_summary: Jesus teaches honest speech, creative nonviolence, and love for enemies as true righteousness.
big_idea: Enemy Love: Because Jesus calls us to love our enemies like the Father does, let’s respond to hurt with creative goodness instead of payback this week.

one_sentence_summary: Devotion isn’t a performance—Jesus calls for secret faithfulness rather than public applause.
big_idea: Sincere Devotion: Because devotion is not a performance, let’s practice one act of giving or prayer in secret for God alone this week.

one_sentence_summary: Jesus teaches a prayer that trains our desires toward God’s Kingdom and daily trust.
big_idea: The Lord’s Prayer: Because this prayer reshapes our desires toward God’s Kingdom, let’s pray it slowly and sincerely each day this week.

one_sentence_summary: Trust the Father’s care, loosen money’s grip, and seek God’s Kingdom first.
big_idea: Wealth & Worry: Because the Father knows what we need, let’s seek His Kingdom first and release one worry or money-grip this week.

one_sentence_summary: Jesus calls for humility, discernment, and mercy in how we treat and judge others.
big_idea: Wisdom in Relationships: Because Jesus calls us to humble, mercy-filled relationships, let’s deal with our own ‘plank’ first and treat others with grace this week.

one_sentence_summary: Jesus ends with a decision: build your life on his words or collapse when storms come.
big_idea: The Choice: Because a life built on Jesus’ words stands in the storms, let’s choose one teaching from this series to obey this week.

one_sentence_summary: This foundational description of God becomes one of the Bible’s most repeated passages.
big_idea: Character of God: Because God reveals His name and character as compassionate and faithful, let’s trust who He is and live in response this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s compassion is deep parental care that moves him to act for his people.
big_idea: Compassion: Because God is compassionate like a father, let’s receive His compassion and show it to someone hurting this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s grace is generous favor given to people who don’t earn it.
big_idea: Grace: Because God’s grace gives what we don’t deserve, let’s accept His gift and extend grace to one person this week.

one_sentence_summary: God is patient—he holds back anger to make room for repentance and restoration.
big_idea: Slow to Anger: Because God is slow to anger, let’s pause before reacting and choose patience in one tense moment this week.

one_sentence_summary: God’s loyal love is covenant commitment—love that keeps showing up.
big_idea: Loyal Love: Because God’s loyal love never quits, let’s keep our commitments and show steadfast love in one relationship this week.

one_sentence_summary: God is utterly trustworthy—steady and true in every season.
big_idea: Faithful: Because God is faithful and trustworthy, let’s step out in obedience on one promise He’s calling us to believe this week.
